questions.question: Solve for x: |x - 4| = 9
questions.explanation: Split into two cases: x - 4 = 9 gives x = 13, and x - 4 = -9 gives x = -5. Check: |13 - 4| = 9 ✓ and |-5 - 4| = 9 ✓

questions.question: Solve for x: |2x + 3| ≤ 7
questions.explanation: Rewrite as -7 ≤ 2x + 3 ≤ 7. Subtract 3: -10 ≤ 2x ≤ 4. Divide by 2: -5 ≤ x ≤ 2.

questions.question: A temperature sensor has a maximum error of |T - 68| ≤ 2, where T is the actual temperature in degrees Fahrenheit. What is the range of possible actual temperatures?
questions.explanation: The inequality |T - 68| ≤ 2 means the actual temperature is at most 2 degrees away from 68. This gives 66 ≤ T ≤ 70.

questions.question: Solve for x: |x + 5| = |2x - 1|
questions.explanation: Case 1: x + 5 = 2x - 1 gives -6 = x, so x = -4. Case 2: x + 5 = -(2x - 1) gives 3x = -4, so x = -4/3. Wait, recalculate Case 2: x + 5 = -2x + 1 gives 3x = -4, x = -4/3. Actually check Case 2 again: if x+5 = -(2x-1), then x+5 = -2x+1, so 3x = -4, x = -4/3. Let me verify: |−4/3+5| = |11/3| = 11/3 and |2(−4/3)−1| = |−8/3−1| = |−11/3| = 11/3 ✓. But recheck Case 1: x+5 = 2x−1 gives 6 = x. |6+5| = 11 and |2(6)−1| = 11 ✓

questions.question: For which values of x is |x - 3| > 5?
questions.explanation: Split the inequality: x - 3 > 5 gives x > 8, and x - 3 < -5 gives x < -2. The solution is the union of these two regions.

questions.question: Solve for x: |3x - 6| + 2 = 11
questions.explanation: Isolate the absolute value: |3x - 6| = 9. Then 3x - 6 = 9 gives x = 5, and 3x - 6 = -9 gives x = -1. Check: |3(5) - 6| + 2 = 9 + 2 = 11 ✓
